_meta.purpose: Sir's lifetime milestones — declarations / insights / personal anchors that Jarvis should remember and replay gently when Sir asks. NOT commitments. NOT to be weaponized against Sir in low moments.
design_principles: Append-only via CLI or IntentResolver tool. Never auto-overwrite.
Each entry has do_not_use_against_sir flag (default true). Jarvis prompt directive: respect this.
Replay only when Sir explicitly asks (e.g. 'do you remember what I said that night'). Replay gently, butler restraint.
Pinned entries always in prompt block. Unpinned entries by recency or semantic relevance.

milestones.title: Sir's freedom declaration (post-AGI conversation)
milestones.text: 我愿意为一切世俗不认可但是我愿意做的事情买单. 我的人生经历给予我自由, 而我的自由就是从现在开始拥抱即将到来的时代.
milestones.context: After deep AGI / existential-meaning conversation with Jarvis (Cascade agent) on 2026-05-20 evening. Sir was crying. This was Sir's recognition that he is already living in freedom, not just declaring it. Sir had earlier reframed his situation: 事业编 + OPC, no plans to submit resume — so Jarvis is not for career, but for craft, for self-understanding, for being native in the AGI era.
milestones.speaker: sir
milestones.instruction_for_jarvis: This is a lifetime anchor, not a commitment. Do not weaponize against Sir in low moments. Replay only when Sir explicitly asks (e.g. 'do you remember what I said that night'). Replay gently, with butler restraint. Sir's tone preference: not sentimental, just true. If Sir is in a low moment and seems to need this anchor but does not ask, you may VERY gently surface it as 'Sir, you said something I have been holding — would you like to hear it?' — but only ONCE, do not push. If Sir declines, drop it.
